title: Schedules

emptyState.title: No schedules yet
emptyState.description: Run an agent automatically on a cron schedule.

noSchedules: No schedules configured. Add a CMATE.md with a Schedules section.
noSchedulesStep2: Click the CMATE button in the toolbar to create CMATE.md
noSchedulesStep3: Edit the Schedules table (Name, Cron, Message, CLI Tool, Enabled, Permission)
activeSchedulesDescription: In-memory cron state currently registered by the scheduler.
noActiveSchedules: No schedules are currently registered in memory.
selectAgents: Select 2 agents to display in the terminal header tabs.
agentInstancesDescription: Manage the agents shown in the terminal header tabs. Add multiple instances of the same tool and give each an alias.
agentInstanceMax: Up to {max} instances can be added.
mobileVisibleInstancesDescription: Choose which instances appear as tabs on this device. This selection is saved per device and does not change the shared instance list.
mobileVisibleInstanceMin: At least one instance must stay visible on this device.
ollamaNotAvailable: Ollama is not running. Start Ollama to select a model.
cmateCreated: CMATE.md created with template. Edit it to configure schedules.

edit.name: Name
edit.askAiHint: Drafts a prompt into the AI chat (no auto-send)

aiPrompts.cron: I want to express the schedule timing as a cron expression. Convert a plain-language request (e.g. "every Monday at 9") into a cron expression and suggest a few candidates (e.g. "every Monday at 9" → `0 9 * * 1`).
aiPrompts.cronRefine: I want to express the schedule timing as a cron expression. The current input is {current}. Building on that, convert a plain-language request (e.g. "every Monday at 9") into a cron expression and suggest a few candidates (e.g. "every Monday at 9" → `0 9 * * 1`).
aiPrompts.messageDraft: For this schedule, I want to write the message (the instruction prompt) that the CLI tool will run on a schedule. First ask me what it is for, then propose the message content based on my answer.
aiPrompts.messageDraftNamed: For the schedule {name}, I want to write the message (the instruction prompt) that the CLI tool will run on a schedule. First ask me what it is for, then propose the message content based on my answer.

edit.errorNameTooLong: Name must be {max} characters or less.
edit.errorMessageTooLong: Message must be {max} characters or less.

timer.title: Delayed Messages
timer.sessionWarning: Session is not running. If the session is not running when the timer fires, the message will not be sent.
timer.clearConfirm: Delete all sent, failed, and cancelled timer history?

memoEmptyHint: Click the button below to add one.
